summary: Replace intervention-heavy and audit-heavy protected follow-up wording in the Site Dashboard and Review Queue helper paths, then guard it with the functionality-language verifier.
selectedReason: Current repo evidence still showed live protected-surface strings in source helpers instead of just stale docs: `Teacher intervention` was emitted from the site dashboard and Review Queue helpers, and the Site Dashboard still described protected access as logged audit activity. This was the smallest safe batch that improved real staff-facing language across existing protected workflows while adding a direct regression guard.

candidateTable.candidate: Site dashboard and Review Queue follow-up language hardening

candidateTable.decision: not selected: broader than the live helper-string wording regression
deferred: no site-backed audit destination is proven yet
deferred: backend queue rows are still absent
deferred: mutation and audit design still needed
deferred: hosted fake-account runtime is outside this local batch
deferred: broader workflow than this language hardening slice

changes: Changed `functions/api/site/dashboard.ts` follow-up labels from `Teacher intervention` to `Teacher follow-up` and replaced the audit-heavy protected-access summary with protected-and-reviewed school wording.
Changed the high-risk Review Queue helper in `functions/_lib/site-review-queue.ts` from `Prioritize teacher intervention.` to `Prioritize teacher follow-up.`
Extended `scripts/verify-functionality-language.mjs` to scan the site-dashboard and Review Queue helper source files and fail on `Teacher intervention` or the retired dashboard audit phrase.
Expanded `tests/workspace-app.test.mjs` and `tests/functionality-language-audit.test.mjs` to prove the rendered copy and verifier coverage.
nextRecommended: Add dashboard and Review Queue risk explanation copy, or add a site-backed recent-activity destination only if the destination is proven and authorized.
doNotRepeat: Do not reintroduce `Teacher intervention` or `Dashboard access is logged for protected school records.` on protected Site Dashboard or Review Queue surfaces unless product language policy intentionally changes.
